comment.base:
A Legacy of Broken Promises and Betrayed Oaths

To Donald Trump and those who now follow in his footsteps, taking the oath of office carries a solemn responsibility: to defend the Constitution and serve the people with integrity. Trump himself took that same oath, yet his presidency stands as a cautionary tale of what happens when personal ambition eclipses duty.

He promised to “drain the swamp,” yet filled it with corruption and self-serving cronies. He vowed to be a president “for all Americans,” but sowed unprecedented division. He spoke of bringing respect to the United States, yet left office with the world viewing him as a symbol of chaos and incompetence.

His broken promises are too numerous to count: failing to deliver meaningful healthcare reform, alienating allies while cozying up to dictators, and leaving a trail of legal and moral failures that culminated in him becoming a felon. The weight of his lies and divisiveness has tarnished not only his legacy but also America’s global standing.

Now, as his influence continues to fester, we are witnessing an unsettling loyalty to a man who has betrayed every principle of leadership. Instead of moving forward, his supporters cling to the rhetoric of a man who has made America a laughingstock, not “great again.”

The oath of office is not just words. It’s a promise to uphold truth, justice, and democracy. Trump’s failure to honor that oath should serve as a stark reminder of what happens when power is abused. The question for his followers now is this: Will you uphold the oath’s true meaning, or will you let his legacy of failure define you?

America deserves leaders who lift it up, not tear it down. History will not forget those who choose the latter.

comment.base:
I am a right-wing citizen of Korea.

Now many right-wing citizens are receiving indiscriminate threats to their lives from the extreme left, the Democratic Party, the Democratic Federation of Trade Unions, and the spies who infiltrated the Republic of Korea. In addition, Lee Jae-myung, a politician of the Democratic Party, was on trial for corruption in the Daejang-dong redevelopment project when he was the mayor of Seongnam, and seven people he worked with died mysteriously. Another example is Yoon Mi-hyang, a member of the same Democratic Party. He was an activist who made the Korean Peninsula comfort women issue and the girl statue known to the world in the Japanese colonial period, but he was brought to trial on suspicion of embezzling donations from Japanese military comfort women grandmothers. Meanwhile, the judge in charge of the trial died suddenly of a heart attack in the restaurant bathroom. Is it a coincidence that Lee Jae-myung and Yoon Mi-hyang belong to the same far-left community? The Democratic Party, a far-left party, has another organization besides the government of the Republic of Korea. So President Yoon Seok-yeol was unable to do normal politics in the Republic of Korea, and the big wall of fraudulent elections led to the declaration of martial law. The Democratic Party, a far-left party that accused Yoon Seok-yeol of being a civil war criminal (can the president himself exercise martial law for the purpose of civil war? The truth is that he declared martial law for the investigation of fraudulent elections), used the investigation office of high-ranking officials to illegally invade the president's residence and investigate the president and the deputy director of the guard's office on charges of illegal civil war. Yoon Seok-yeol voluntarily attended the investigator's office for the investigation to prevent a bloodshed between the bodyguard and the high-ranking public official. Yoon Seok-yeol refused to make a statement in silence knowing the illegal convenience warrant issued by the same cartel group in the Seoul West District Court. On January 18, Judge Cha Eun-kyung of the Seoul West District Court, the same side as the Cartel Group, issued an arrest warrant.

As a result, there was an incident in which a young right-wing citizen rioted in the court due to the incitement of the left wing, and this

The cartel group is putting more pressure on right-wing citizens who rioted with President Yoon Seok-yeol, and they are abandoning their party to sentence young right-wing citizens to 10 years in prison. Therefore, right-wing citizens are fighting peacefully with the cartel group without wavering. Right-wing citizens want to actively intervene in the United States. President Yoon Seok-yeol is in danger of impeachment by a constitutional trial that has already been decided. There is no doubt that President Yoon Seok-yeol will greatly help the interests of the United States.

You can't look at Korea from the same perspective as South Vietnam. It will be communistized like Vietnam, the second country on the Korean Peninsula, and many right-wing citizens will turn away from the United States and lose right-wing citizens who helped the United States. As an ordinary citizen of the Republic of Korea, I am not a powerful politician, so I tell the truth to the conservative American journalist Fox News. I hope American citizens and politicians know that this message is true.
